questions.explanation: `top` provides an interactive, real-time view of processes and system resource usage.
questions.question: In Linux, which command is commonly used to view real-time system and process resource usage (CPU/memory) interactively?

questions.explanation: A Deployment manages ReplicaSets/Pods and supports declarative updates like rolling deployments and rollbacks.

options.B: Manage a replicated set of Pods and perform rolling updates
options.C: Expose a Pod to external traffic via a cloud load balancer
options.D: Schedule Pods onto nodes based on taints and tolerations

questions.question: In Kubernetes, what is the primary purpose of a Deployment?

questions.explanation: Terraform state tracks resource mappings and metadata so Terraform can compute diffs and manage infrastructure safely.

options.A: To store application logs for debugging
options.B: To define cloud IAM policies in JSON
options.C: To map declared resources to real infrastructure and track their current attributes
options.D: To compile HCL into a binary plan executable

questions.question: In Terraform, what is the purpose of the state file?

questions.explanation: DMAIC is the standard Six Sigma improvement framework: Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control.
questions.question: In Six Sigma, what does the 'DMAIC' cycle stand for?

questions.explanation: Autoscaling plus right-sizing matches capacity to demand, reducing waste while maintaining availability through scaling policies.

options.A: Overprovision instances to avoid scaling events
options.B: Use autoscaling with right-sized instances and set scale-down policies based on utilization
options.C: Disable multi-AZ deployments to reduce redundancy costs
options.D: Pin workloads to the largest instance type to reduce management overhead

questions.question: You want to reduce cloud costs for a stateless web service with variable traffic. Which approach is typically most effective without sacrificing availability?

questions.explanation: Canary releases limit blast radius and, with automated rollback tied to SLO/error signals, reduce risk and time-to-mitigate.

options.A: Run unit tests only after deployment to production
options.B: Use a canary deployment with automated rollback based on SLO/error-rate signals
options.C: Deploy all changes manually to ensure human oversight
options.D: Skip integration tests to shorten pipeline duration

questions.question: In CI/CD, which practice best reduces the risk of deploying a breaking change to production?

questions.explanation: OpenTelemetry tracing relies on context propagation (commonly W3C Trace Context) and spans to represent operations across services.

options.A: Generate a new trace ID for every log line and store it only in the log message text
options.B: Propagate context via standard headers (e.g., W3C Trace Context) and create spans around key operations
options.C: Disable sampling to ensure every request is traced at all times in production
options.D: Send traces to Prometheus because it is the standard tracing backend

questions.question: You are instrumenting a Python service for distributed tracing across microservices. Which approach is most aligned with OpenTelemetry best practices?

questions.explanation: Traces pinpoint failing/slow dependencies and, when correlated with logs, quickly distinguishes app errors from downstream failures.

options.A: Increase the Deployment replica count immediately
options.B: Check distributed traces to identify which downstream span(s) have increased latency/errors and correlate with logs by trace_id
options.C: Restart all nodes in the cluster to clear potential kernel issues
options.D: Disable the alert to reduce on-call noise

questions.question: A Prometheus alert 'HighErrorRate' is firing for a Kubernetes service. Grafana shows a spike in 5xx responses, but node CPU/memory are normal. Which next step most directly helps isolate whether the issue is application-level vs. dependency-level?

questions.explanation: SLO burn rate shows urgency, and version-based segmentation attributes whether the new release caused the regression despite traffic changes.

options.A: Compare absolute error counts only; if higher, roll back immediately
options.B: Evaluate SLO burn rate (error budget consumption) and segment metrics by release version (e.g., canary vs. baseline) to attribute impact
options.C: Ignore latency because only availability matters
options.D: Wait for the incident to resolve on its own to avoid unnecessary changes

questions.question: During an incident, you need to decide whether to roll back a new release. Error rate increased from 0.2% to 1.5% and p95 latency doubled, but traffic also increased 3x due to a marketing event. Which analysis is most appropriate for a dependability engineer before rolling back?

questions.explanation: Predictive maintenance is time-dependent and imbalanced; time-based validation avoids leakage, and recall/PR-AUC with cost-based thresholds addresses rare, high-impact failures.

options.A: Use accuracy as the primary metric and choose the model with the highest accuracy
options.B: Use a random train/test split without considering time ordering
options.C: Use time-based cross-validation and prioritize recall (sensitivity) and precision/PR-AUC, selecting an operating threshold based on cost of false negatives
options.D: Optimize only for mean squared error (MSE) regardless of class imbalance

questions.question: You are building a predictive maintenance model for rotating equipment using vibration and temperature sensors. Failures are rare, and missing a failure is very costly. Which evaluation approach is most appropriate?
